requirements.text: WHEN user activates quick search, the system shall present a visible scope indicator showing the active search scope (Global, Tickets, Projects, or Documents)
WHEN user activates quick search, the system shall display visible scope controls allowing selection of Global, Tickets, Projects, or Documents scope
WHEN user activates a scope control, the system shall switch the search scope to the selected entity type and update the scope indicator within 100ms
WHEN user types a query in Global scope, the system shall display results grouped into separate labeled sections for Projects, Tickets, and Documents
WHEN search results include multiple entity types, the system shall render each entity type with visually distinct styling (icon, color, or badge)
WHEN user selects a project result, the system shall navigate to the selected project and close the search modal
WHEN user selects a ticket result, the system shall navigate to the selected ticket and close the search modal
WHEN user selects a document result, the system shall navigate to the selected document and close the search modal
WHEN user types a project code query, the system shall match and return projects whose code matches the query as a case-insensitive prefix
WHEN user types an exact ticket key (e.g., 'MDT-179'), the system shall prioritize the ticket lookup over project and document matching
WHEN user types a project-scoped ticket query (e.g., '@CODE text'), the system shall search for tickets within the specified project and display results as ticket results, not project results
WHEN search scope is set to Projects, the system shall display only project results matching the query
WHEN search scope is set to Tickets, the system shall display only ticket results matching the query
WHEN search scope is set to Documents, the system shall display only document results matching the query
WHEN user switches search scope, the system shall either preserve the current query text or clear it according to a visible predictable rule displayed to the user
WHEN keyboard focus is inside the search modal, Arrow Up and Arrow Down keys shall navigate across all visible result items in grouped sections sequentially
WHEN keyboard focus is inside the search modal, Tab key shall jump to the first item of the next result group and Shift+Tab shall jump to the first item of the previous group
WHEN user presses Enter on a selected result, the system shall activate that result (navigate to project/ticket/document) and close the modal
WHEN a query matches both a project name and a ticket title, the system shall display separate project results and ticket results in their respective groups
WHEN search results are empty for the active scope, the system shall display a no-results state that identifies the active scope and does not imply other scopes were searched
WHEN user enters invalid project-code syntax in the project scope, the system shall not block project name lookup
Search scope changes shall not cause visible layout jumps in the command palette (reflow must be bounded to content area)
Result grouping shall remain readable on viewports from 320px to 1920px width
Keyboard focus shall remain inside the command palette while it is open
Search shall not reveal entities unavailable to the current access mode (read-only sessions, token-scoped sessions)
Existing ticket search behavior (current_project mode, ticket_key mode, project_scope mode) shall be preserved without breaking changes
The search interaction model shall be extensible to document content search without requiring a command palette redesign
WHEN a query matches only hidden or inaccessible entities, the system shall not expose those entity names in search results
WHEN user types a partial project name query (e.g., 'task ma'), the system shall match and return projects whose name contains word prefixes matching each query term
WHEN user presses Tab (or Shift+Tab) in the search modal, the system shall cycle the search scope (All → Tickets → Projects → Documents) without requiring pointer input

scenarios.title: Scope controls are visible when quick search opens
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is opened via Cmd+K
scenarios.then: a scope indicator shows 'Global' as active scope AND scope control buttons/tabs for Global, Tickets, Projects, and Documents are visible

scenarios.title: User switches scope by clicking a scope control
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open with Global scope active
scenarios.when: the user clicks the 'Projects' scope control
scenarios.then: the scope indicator updates to 'Projects' within 100ms AND the results area filters to show only project-type results

scenarios.title: Global search displays grouped results for all entity types
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open in Global scope
scenarios.when: the user types a query matching at least one project, one ticket, and one document
scenarios.then: results are displayed in three separate labeled groups (Projects, Tickets, Documents) AND each group has visually distinct styling (icon, color, or badge)

scenarios.title: Exact ticket key prioritizes ticket lookup
scenarios.given: a ticket with key 'MDT-179' exists AND a project containing 'MDT' in its name exists
scenarios.when: the user types 'MDT-179' in the search input
scenarios.then: the ticket result for MDT-179 appears with higher prominence than project results

scenarios.title: Arrow keys navigate across grouped sections, Tab jumps between groups
scenarios.given: the quick search modal shows grouped results with Projects (2 items) and Tickets (3 items)
scenarios.when: the user presses ArrowDown from the last project item
scenarios.then: focus moves to the first ticket item AND pressing Tab from the Tickets group jumps focus to the first item of the Projects group

scenarios.title: Enter activates the selected result and closes modal
scenarios.given: the quick search modal shows grouped results AND a project result is selected via keyboard
scenarios.then: the modal closes AND the application navigates to the selected project

scenarios.title: Query matching project name and ticket title shows separate groups
scenarios.given: a project named 'Test' exists AND a ticket titled 'Test feature' exists
scenarios.when: the user types 'test' in the search input
scenarios.then: the project appears in the Projects group AND the ticket appears in the Tickets group as separate, labeled results

scenarios.title: No-results state identifies the active scope
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open in Projects scope
scenarios.when: the user types a query that matches no projects
scenarios.then: a no-results message shows the active scope as 'Projects' AND does not imply tickets or documents were searched

scenarios.title: Scoped search filters to one entity type and preserves query on scope switch
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open
scenarios.when: the user types a query matching projects and tickets, then switches scope from Global to Projects
scenarios.then: the query text is preserved AND only project results are displayed

scenarios.title: Project-scoped ticket query returns tickets and invalid code does not block project name lookup
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open in Projects scope
scenarios.when: the user types an invalid project-code pattern followed by a name
scenarios.then: the system performs project name lookup without blocking on the invalid code syntax

scenarios.title: Selecting a ticket result navigates to that ticket
scenarios.given: the quick search modal shows grouped results including tickets
scenarios.when: the user clicks a ticket result
scenarios.then: the search modal closes AND the application navigates to the ticket

scenarios.title: Selecting a document result navigates to that document
scenarios.given: the quick search modal shows grouped results including documents
scenarios.when: the user clicks a document result
scenarios.then: the search modal closes AND the application navigates to the document

scenarios.title: Project-scoped ticket query (@CODE text) returns ticket results not project results
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open
scenarios.when: the user types '@MDT search term' to search tickets in the MDT project
scenarios.then: results display tickets from the MDT project in a Tickets section, not the MDT project itself

scenarios.title: Selecting a project result navigates to that project
scenarios.given: the quick search modal shows grouped results including a project named 'Task Manager'
scenarios.when: the user clicks the project result for 'Task Manager'
scenarios.then: the search modal closes AND the application navigates to the 'Task Manager' project

scenarios.title: Partial project name query matches by word prefix
scenarios.given: a project named 'Task Manager' with code 'TMGR' exists
scenarios.when: the user types 'task ma' in the search input
scenarios.then: the 'Task Manager' project appears in the Projects result group

scenarios.title: User switches scope via Tab key
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open with any scope active
scenarios.when: the user presses the Tab key
scenarios.then: the scope cycles to the next tab (All→Tickets→Projects→Documents→All)

scenarios.title: Repeated Tab presses cycle scopes with visible indicator; Shift+Tab reverses
scenarios.given: the quick search modal is open on Global scope
scenarios.when: the user presses Tab multiple times
scenarios.then: the scope cycles through All→Tickets→Projects→Documents→All and the active tab indicator updates each time; Shift+Tab cycles backwards

obligations.title: Create UnifiedSearchRequest and UnifiedSearchResponse schemas supporting discriminated result types (project, ticket, document) alongside existing SearchResponse for backward compatibility
Restructure QuickSearchResults to render N labeled result groups with per-entity-type row components (ProjectResultRow, DocumentResultRow, existing ticket row)
Add SearchScopeBar component with tab/button group for scope selection and integrate into QuickSearchInput
Add client-side project matching hook (useProjectSearch) with word-prefix scoring using existing projects prop
Create new /api/search endpoint with SearchController for unified search across entity types, preserving existing /api/projects/search endpoint
Unified search must filter results by current session access policy, excluding hidden/unavailable projects, tickets, and documents
No-results states must identify the active scope and not imply other scopes were searched

Extend QueryMode from 3 ticket modes to 6+ modes (global, projects, documents, tickets, ticket_key, project_scope) with scope-aware classification; extend useCrossProjectSearch to use unified search endpoint when available
Introduce SearchScope enum (Global, Tickets, Projects, Documents) and scope state management via useSearchScope hook
Arrow keys navigate all results across grouped sections; Tab cycles scope tabs (All→Tickets→Projects→Documents); Shift+Tab reverses; Enter activates selected result

artifacts.sourceRef: Architecture: New unified search types module
Architecture: New scope state management hook
Architecture: Restructured modal with scope integration
Architecture: Grouped results with entity type sections
Architecture: Scope selector tab bar component
Architecture: New unified search API route

testPlans.title: useProjectSearch hook tests - client-side project matching
Extended query mode parsing with scope-aware classification
Keyboard navigation across N grouped sections
Unified search API endpoint integration tests

tasks.title: Grouped results rendering with entity type row components
Unified search backend endpoint with access control
Walking skeleton: search types, scope enum, schemas, and tests
Scope state hook, scope bar component, and scope bar tests
Keyboard navigation across N grouped sections and modal integration
Client-side project matching and extended query mode
